title: amaryllidaceae
intro: The taxonomic rank of Amaryllidaceae is a family of flowering plants. It is a diverse group of monocots that includes approximately 75 genera and over 1,000 species, commonly known as the amaryllis family. Amaryllidaceae plants are characterized by their showy, often fragrant flowers and strap-like or linear leaves, and are found in a wide range of habitats, including tropical and subtropical regions. This family includes well-known genera such as Hippeastrum, Galtonia, and Crinum.
